_meta.purpose: DEMO DATASET destinations/landmarks for Village Green community-aid app (rides/errands/help). Affectionate, witty, Rhode-Island-themed. Real places use real towns; Miskatonic Health network is invented (corporate-yet-eldritch, mirroring Brown University Health).

destinations.name: Bally's Twin River Lincoln
destinations.note: Former horse track turned 3,900-slot palace; drop Nana at the nickel slots and she will not be ready to leave when you said.

destinations.name: Bally's Tiverton Casino Hotel
destinations.note: Built a stone's throw from the Massachusetts line, expressly so Rhode Islanders could keep their gambling money in-state.

destinations.name: Newport Grand (former Newport Jai Alai)
destinations.note: Once you bet on grown men hurling a pelota at 180 mph; then it was slots; now it's mostly memories and parking.

destinations.name: Lincoln Greyhound / Old Lincoln Downs site
destinations.note: Where it all started in 1947 before video lottery terminals quietly ate the racetrack.

destinations.name: The Breakers
destinations.note: Cornelius Vanderbilt II's 70-room 'summer cottage'; the Great Hall is 45 feet tall because subtlety is for poor people.

destinations.name: Marble House
destinations.note: An $11 million 39th-birthday present in 500,000 cubic feet of marble; nothing says 'I love you' like geology.

destinations.name: The Elms
destinations.note: Coal baron's French chateau, one of the first Newport houses fully electrified, with a sunken garden you could lose a grandchild in.

destinations.name: Rosecliff
destinations.note: Silver-heiress ballroom modeled on Versailles' Grand Trianon; you've seen it in The Great Gatsby, which tracks.

destinations.name: Chateau-sur-Mer
destinations.note: The big Bellevue Ave Victorian from before the Gilded Age went fully feral; restrained, for a mansion.

destinations.name: Belcourt of Newport
destinations.note: Oliver Belmont's horse-obsessed palace where the stables got the good rooms; privately owned, gloriously eccentric.

destinations.name: Rough Point
destinations.note: Doris Duke's clifftop English manor, complete with the camels she reportedly kept; tobacco money, kept weird.

destinations.name: The Conjuring House (Old Arnold Estate)
destinations.note: The 1736 Perron-family farmhouse on Round Top Road; now you can pay to spend the night, which is a choice.

destinations.name: The Secret Mall Apartment
destinations.note: Eight artists secretly lived in a 750-sq-ft void inside Providence Place Mall for four years; the Netflix doc made them folk heroes.

destinations.name: Mercy Brown's Grave, Chestnut Hill Cemetery
destinations.note: RI's most famous 'vampire'; her heart was burned in 1892 and possibly inspired Dracula's Lucy. Bring a penny, leave the heart.

destinations.name: Swan Point Cemetery (Lovecraft's grave)
destinations.note: H.P. Lovecraft rests under 'I AM PROVIDENCE'; fans leave pens, plastic tentacles, and existential dread.

destinations.name: Ladd Observatory
destinations.note: Young Lovecraft's gateway to cosmic horror; he peered through the telescope and decided the universe hates us. Open Tuesday nights.

destinations.name: Fleur-de-Lys Studios
destinations.note: The 1885 Arts-and-Crafts oddity that Lovecraft made sculptor Wilcox's home in 'The Call of Cthulhu.' Eldritch but charming.

destinations.name: The Shunned House
destinations.note: The Benefit Street home behind Lovecraft's tale of a cellar-dwelling French horror; please don't disturb the mold.

destinations.name: Ramtail Factory Ruins
destinations.note: Officially listed as haunted by the 1885 RI census, the only one; night-watchman Peleg Walker still allegedly rings the bell he hanged from.

destinations.name: The Big Blue Bug (Nibbles Woodaway)
destinations.note: A 58-foot, 4,000-lb fiberglass termite over I-95, named by a Tiverton woman in a contest; gets dressed up for the holidays.

destinations.name: Hasbro HQ / Mr. Potato Head
destinations.note: Home of the first toy advertised on TV; the official State Children's Ambassador is a plastic spud, and we are proud.

destinations.name: Crescent Park Looff Carousel
destinations.note: An 1895 National Historic Landmark with 61 hand-carved horses, sole survivor of the 'Coney Island of the East.' Still grab the brass ring.

destinations.name: Rhode Island State House / The Independent Man
destinations.note: Fourth-largest stone dome on earth, topped by a 1,200-lb gilded fella who recently came down for a once-in-50-years cleaning.

destinations.name: WaterFire Providence
destinations.note: Eighty braziers set ablaze in the rivers downtown with haunting music; somehow both a fire hazard and the most romantic night of your life.

destinations.name: Federal Hill / La Pigna Arch
destinations.note: The Atwells Avenue gateway hung with a giant bronze pinecone (locals say 'the pineapple'); cross under it for cannoli and atmosphere.

destinations.name: Del's Frozen Lemonade
destinations.note: Slushy lemon ice with real rind, served year-round; do NOT ask for a spoon or a straw unless you enjoy being judged.

destinations.name: Newport Creamery
destinations.note: Home of the Awful Awful ('awful big, awful good'); finish three in one sitting and the next is free. People have tried.

destinations.name: Iggy's Doughboys & Chowder House
destinations.note: Doughboys, clamcakes, and chowder by the water; sugar-dusted fried dough is a perfectly acceptable lunch here.

destinations.name: Autocrat / Coffee Milk Country
destinations.note: They brew the coffee syrup behind RI's official state drink; drive George Washington Highway at the right hour and the whole road smells like dessert.

destinations.name: Olneyville N.Y. System
destinations.note: Hot wieners (a.k.a. 'gaggahs') 'all the way' with meat sauce, onion, celery salt, mustard; ordered 'up the arm,' lined along the cook's forearm.

destinations.name: Allie's Donuts
destinations.note: Hand-cut since 1968 on Quaker Lane; cash only, closed Mondays and Tuesdays, worth rearranging your whole week for.

destinations.name: Aunt Carrie's (clamcakes & chowder / stuffies)
destinations.note: Shore-dinner institution where the clamcakes, chowder, and stuffies are an entire personality. Bring elastic-waist pants.

destinations.name: Beavertail State Park & Lighthouse
destinations.note: Granite cliffs, crashing surf, and America's third-oldest lighthouse site (1749); the best free drama in the state.

destinations.name: Block Island / Southeast Light
destinations.note: A ferry ride to a brick-red clifftop lighthouse and Mohegan Bluffs; bring a bike, leave the car, lose the cell signal.

destinations.name: Roger Williams Park & Zoo
destinations.note: Victorian park with a zoo, carousel, botanical center, and the Jack-O-Lantern Spectacular every fall; the elephants are locals now.

destinations.name: The Cliff Walk
destinations.note: Three-and-a-half miles where the Gilded Age mansions on one side meet the Atlantic chewing the rocks on the other; mind the loose railings.

destinations.name: Lincoln Woods State Park
destinations.note: Glacial boulders, a swimming pond, and the closest thing to wilderness within a Dunkin's drive of Providence.

destinations.name: Gaspee Point / Pawtuxet Village
destinations.note: Where colonists torched HMS Gaspee in 1772, arguably the first blow of the Revolution; celebrated each June with Gaspee Days.

destinations.name: Ancients & Horribles Parade
destinations.note: A gleefully satirical Fourth of July parade where floats roast politicians; small-town RI at its most lovingly unhinged.

destinations.name: Old Slater Mill
destinations.note: The 1793 water-powered cotton mill where the American Industrial Revolution started, and arguably where industrial espionage did too.

destinations.name: Fort Adams State Park
destinations.note: Massive 19th-century coastal fort that now hosts the Newport Jazz and Folk Festivals; cannons by day, Dylan covers by night.

destinations.name: Touro Synagogue
destinations.note: The oldest synagogue building in North America (1763) and the address of Washington's famous letter on religious liberty.

destinations.name: Providence Athenaeum
destinations.note: A jewel-box 1838 membership library where Edgar Allan Poe courted Sarah Helen Whitman; quietest place to dodge your relatives.

destinations.name: Providence Place Mall
destinations.note: Three floors of retail, a food court, and one very famous formerly-secret apartment; park on the right level or you'll never be seen again.

destinations.name: Garden City Center
destinations.note: Open-air shopping center and unofficial town green; where you run 'one quick errand' and emerge two hours later with a pretzel.

destinations.name: RI DMV (Division of Motor Vehicles)
destinations.note: The Forum-style purgatory off Pontiac Ave; take a number, bring a snack, abandon all hope of being out by lunch.

destinations.name: Dave's Marketplace
destinations.note: The beloved RI grocery chain for stuffies, clamcakes, and the good deli; where 'I'm just grabbing milk' goes to die.

destinations.name: Stop & Shop
destinations.note: The big weekly grocery run; reliable spot for a ride request when the trunk needs to hold a month of canned goods.

destinations.name: The Local Barber Shop
destinations.note: Where the haircut takes twenty minutes and the political analysis takes an hour; a vital community institution.

destinations.name: The Paint Store / Hardware
destinations.note: Color-match the porch, rent the sander, get unsolicited advice from three retired contractors. Classic errand run.

destinations.name: Neighborhood Pharmacy
destinations.note: Prescription pickups and flu shots; the single most-requested ride for our older members, no notes.

destinations.name: Senior Center
destinations.note: Bingo, lunch, tax help, and the social heartbeat of the week; missing the van here is a genuine tragedy.

destinations.name: Dialysis Center / Clinic
destinations.note: Three-times-a-week, non-negotiable, same time every visit; the rides that matter most, run like clockwork.

destinations.name: Providence VA Medical Center
destinations.note: Appointments for our veterans on Chalkstone Ave; allow extra time for parking and even more for swapping stories in the waiting room.

destinations.name: The Polls / Local Voting Precinct
destinations.note: School gym, church basement, or fire station on Election Day; getting members there is the most democratic errand we run.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health – Innsmouth Urgent Care
miskatonicHealth.note: Walk-ins welcome; please describe your symptoms AND any recent dreams of the deep. Now validating parking.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health Heart & Vascular at Kingsport
miskatonicHealth.note: Cardiology for hearts both human and otherwise; ask about our 'still-beating' wellness program.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Imaging – Federal Hill
miskatonicHealth.note: MRI, CT, and ultrasound; results in 48 hours, or sooner if the films develop on their own.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health Surgery Center
miskatonicHealth.note: Same-day outpatient procedures performed by board-certified and reanimation-adjacent staff.

miskatonicHealth.name: The Armitage Pavilion at Miskatonic Health
miskatonicHealth.note: Our flagship inpatient tower, named for the dean who definitely did not read aloud from any books.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health – Dunwich Family Medicine
miskatonicHealth.note: Whole-family primary care; we proudly see patients of all sizes, including the larger twin.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Cancer Institute
miskatonicHealth.note: Comprehensive oncology and infusion; our radiation therapy glows a reassuring non-Euclidean green.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health Behavioral Wellness at Sefton Asylum
miskatonicHealth.note: Compassionate inpatient psychiatry on a historic campus; visiting hours end strictly at dusk.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health – Aylesbury Orthopedics & Spine
miskatonicHealth.note: Joint replacement and spine care; we'll have you walking upright again, mostly.

miskatonicHealth.name: The West Pavilion for Regenerative Medicine
miskatonicHealth.note: Cutting-edge stem-cell and tissue restoration, per the lifelong research of Dr. Herbert W. Results may vary.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Women's Health – Rosecliff
miskatonicHealth.note: OB/GYN and midwifery in a gracious bayside setting; deliveries attended around the clock and around the tide.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health Eye Institute (Whateley Vision)
miskatonicHealth.note: LASIK, cataracts, and specialty optometry; we fit lenses for every eye you happen to have.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Children's at the Curwen Center
miskatonicHealth.note: Pediatric care in a family-friendly space; our colonial-era founder was 'very good with children's records.'

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health – Pickman Wound & Hyperbaric Care
miskatonicHealth.note: Advanced wound healing; our specialists have an uncanny, almost lifelike eye for tissue.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Sleep Disorders Center
miskatonicHealth.note: Overnight studies for apnea, insomnia, and recurring visions of a sunken city. Pillows provided.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health Rehabilitation at Ramtail Mill
miskatonicHealth.note: Inpatient PT and OT in a restored historic mill; our bells ring promptly at shift change, no staff required.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Dialysis – Pawtucket
miskatonicHealth.note: Convenient outpatient hemodialysis; we filter blood of nearly every viscosity and color.

miskatonicHealth.name: The Dexter Tower at Miskatonic Health
miskatonicHealth.note: Our newest patient pavilion; the elevator stops at every floor, including the ones not on the directory.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Primary Care – Innsmouth Wharf
miskatonicHealth.note: Neighborhood clinic by the water; accepting new patients, established families, and the Esoteric Order's group plan.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health Cancer & Infusion at Cliff Walk
miskatonicHealth.note: Oceanview chemo suites because healing deserves a view, even of the indifferent and churning sea.

miskatonicHealth.name: Miskatonic Health Emergency Department – Arkham Main
miskatonicHealth.note: Level I trauma center, open 24/7/∞; current wait time is posted, though time behaves oddly past the triage desk.
